# Example: What /plan Produces

This shows the output of running `/plan add user authentication with JWT` on a Next.js project.

## Generated Files

### `.claude/plans/2026-03-23-user-auth-jwt/prd.md`

```markdown
# PRD: User Authentication with JWT

> Generated by /plan on 2026-03-23

## Overview

Add JWT-based authentication to the application, allowing users to register,
log in, and access protected routes with token-based sessions.

## User Stories

- As a visitor, I want to create an account so I can access the application.
- As a user, I want to log in with email/password so I can resume my session.
- As a user, I want my session to persist across page refreshes.
- As a developer, I want protected API routes to reject unauthenticated requests.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Registration with email + password (hashed with bcrypt)
- [ ] Login returns a JWT token (expires in 7 days)
- [ ] Protected routes return 401 without valid token
- [ ] Token refresh before expiration
- [ ] Logout clears the token

## Technical Requirements

- JWT library: jose (Web Crypto API, no Node.js dependency)
- Password hashing: bcrypt
- Storage: HttpOnly cookies (not localStorage)

## Out of Scope

- OAuth/social login
- Multi-factor authentication
- Password reset flow
```

### `.claude/plans/2026-03-23-user-auth-jwt/architecture.md`

```markdown
# Architecture: User Authentication with JWT

## Data Model / ERD

erDiagram
    USER {
        uuid id PK
        string email UK
        string password_hash
        timestamp created_at
        timestamp updated_at
    }
    SESSION {
        uuid id PK
        uuid user_id FK
        string refresh_token
        timestamp expires_at
    }
    USER ||--o{ SESSION : has

## API Contracts

### POST /api/auth/register
- Request: { email, password }
- Response: { user: { id, email } }
- Errors: 400 (validation), 409 (email exists)

### POST /api/auth/login
- Request: { email, password }
- Response: { user: { id, email } } + HttpOnly cookie
- Errors: 401 (invalid credentials)
```

### `.claude/plans/2026-03-23-user-auth-jwt/plan.md`

```markdown
# Implementation Plan: User Authentication with JWT

## Steps

### Step 1: User model and database schema
- Files: prisma/schema.prisma, src/lib/db.ts
- Tests: Unit test for schema validation
- Complexity: Low

### Step 2: Password hashing utility
- Files: src/lib/auth/password.ts
- Tests: Hash and verify round-trip test
- Complexity: Low

### Step 3: JWT token utilities
- Files: src/lib/auth/jwt.ts
- Tests: Sign, verify, expire tests
- Complexity: Medium

### Step 4: Registration endpoint
- Files: src/app/api/auth/register/route.ts
- Tests: Success, duplicate email, validation errors
- Complexity: Medium

### Step 5: Login endpoint
- Files: src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts
- Tests: Success, wrong password, nonexistent user
- Complexity: Medium

### Step 6: Auth middleware
- Files: src/middleware.ts
- Tests: Valid token, expired token, missing token
- Complexity: Medium
```
